title: Amazon AppFlow Flow Definition
description: Schema defining the structure of an Amazon AppFlow flow resource, including source and destination configurations, task definitions, trigger settings, and flow metadata.

flowName.description: The specified name of the flow.

description.description: A user-entered description of the flow.

flowStatus.description: Indicates the current status of the flow.

destinationFlowConfigList.description: The configuration that controls how Amazon AppFlow places data in the destination connector.

tasks.description: A list of tasks that Amazon AppFlow performs while transferring the data in the flow run.

createdAt.description: Specifies when the flow was created.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Specifies when the flow was last updated.

tags.description: The tags used to organize, track, or control access for your flow.

SourceFlowConfig.description: Contains information about the configuration of the source connector.

connectorType.description: The type of connector, such as Salesforce, Zendesk, Slack, etc.

connectorProfileName.description: The name of the connector profile.

sourceConnectorProperties.description: Specifies the information that is required to query a particular source connector.

DestinationFlowConfig.description: Contains information about the configuration of destination connector.

destinationConnectorProperties.description: The properties that are required to query the destination connector.

Task.description: A class for modeling different type of tasks.

sourceFields.description: The source fields to which a particular task is applied.

connectorOperator.description: The operation to be performed on the provided source fields.

destinationField.description: A field in a destination connector.

TriggerConfig.description: The trigger settings that determine how and when Amazon AppFlow runs the specified flow.

triggerType.description: Specifies the type of flow trigger.

triggerProperties.description: Specifies the configuration details of a schedule-triggered flow.
